parentQuestion: The process by which cells engulf particles or other cells is called ___.

scaffolds.q: Endocytosis is the process of bringing materials ___ the cell.
scaffolds.explain: Endocytosis means 'into the cell' — it's the process by which cells take in materials from their environment by surrounding them with membrane. Endo equals in. Easy, darling.

scaffolds.q: White blood cells engulf and destroy bacteria. This is an example of cells eating ___.
scaffolds.explain: When white blood cells surround and engulf bacteria (solid particles), they are performing a type of endocytosis that involves eating solid material. Your immune cells are literally eating the invaders. Accept this reality:drawn into the violence of it.
